items.title: Anchor vocabulary before mechanisms
items.avoid: Jumping straight into transformer blocks or diffusion math when learners still confuse AI, ML, DL, and Gen AI.
items.prefer: Establish predict-vs-generate and nested taxonomy first, then link out for model-type and LLM depth.
items.why: Correct placement prevents duplicated fundamentals and wrong mental models about "four separate technologies."
items.example: Lesson order: this topic → deep-learning (stack) → what-is-genai (mechanisms) → llms-fundamentals (scale and behavior).

items.title: Separate training paradigm from product label
items.avoid: Saying "self-supervised = Gen AI" or "supervised = not AI."
items.prefer: Teach self-supervised under types-of-learning; teach generative outputs under this topic and what-is-genai.
items.why: Foundation models blur lines—pretraining paradigm and deployment mode are independent axes.
items.example: Instruction-tuned chat (Gen AI product) may sit on a model pretrained self-supervised on web text (types-of-learning--self-supervised).

items.title: Use links instead of copy-paste depth
items.avoid: Re-explaining GANs, VAEs, or RLHF on the taxonomy page.
items.prefer: One-sentence pointer plus topicId link to generative-model-types or llms-fundamentals.
items.why: This repo treats ai-ml-dl-genai--generative-ai as the canonical taxonomy owner, not the fundamentals owner.
items.example: connections.json entry: "Autoregressive, VAE, GAN, diffusion → generative-model-types."
